'''Wann Tsir''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|319|Wann Tsir}}</ref> located in the [[Wann Tsir system/Legends|Wann Tsir system]], a part of the [[Sepan sector/Legends|Sepan sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="EAOC" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Wann Tsir was protected by a [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]].<ref name="CSWE" />

Following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="CSWE" /> of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the planet came under [[Siege of Wann Tsir|siege]]. During the battle, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Titus Klev]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="CSWE" /> accidentally<ref name="DES">''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref> discovered and stopped<ref name="CSWE" /> an important<ref name="CSWE Klev">{{CSWECite|II|221|Klev, Captain Titus}}</ref> [[Unidentified Rebel Alliance agent|agent]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]],<ref name="CSWE" /> who was attempting to [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]]<ref name="DES" /> Wann Tsir's [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]]. Klev's deeds subsequently earned him commendations from the [[Imperial High Command/Legends|Imperial High Command]].<ref name="CSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Wann Tsir was introduced via mention of the Siege of Wann Tsir in the [[1993]] ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', which was authored by [[Michael Allen Horne]] and published by [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="DES" /> It was not until the [[1998]] ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]] was published that it was identified as a distinct astronomical object.<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Wann Tsir [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Wann Tsir itself, in grid square P-13.<ref name="TEA" />
